intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The visual data indicates a critical failure in the biome registry. P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visible as living specimens. The pots appear to contain only soil and debris.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected in black pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss: The Pothos is absent; only soil and the white rabbit anchor remain.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface, no signs of life.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface, no signs of life.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present, but the host plant (Pothos) is missing.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface, no signs of life.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Uniformly dark, potentially saturated or high-organic content, no visible fungal blooms.
biome_observations.debris: White fragments (likely eggshell or perlite) present in P3; electronic sensor probe visible in P1.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no significant organic debris or spillover.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: Across the 5-day sequence, there is zero vegetative progression. The state of the pots remains static, confirming that the specimens were likely already lost or removed prior to the start of this observation window.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: All specimens are confirmed as 'Systemic Loss'. There is no biological activity to evaluate.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a white rabbit anchor in an empty pot (P3) and an electronic sensor in an empty pot (P1) suggests a dormant or abandoned experimental setup.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a complete absence of the expected botanical specimens. The biome is currently non-functional. The presence of the white rabbit anchor and sensor probe indicates this was once an active monitoring site, but the plants have either been removed or have perished entirely, leaving only the substrate and hardware.
